We have that $X = Y \cdot Q = (2 \cdot 5) \cdot 3 = 30$ and $X = Z \cdot R = 7 \cdot (11 \cdot 2) = 154$. Therefore, $30 = 154$, which is a contradiction. However, we can also see that $X = Y \cdot Q = Z \cdot R$ and $Y = Q \cdot 5$ and $Z = R \cdot 11$. Therefore, $X = (Q \cdot 5) \cdot Q = (R \cdot 11) \cdot R$. Therefore, $Q^2 = R^2 \cdot 11$. Therefore, $Q = R \cdot \sqrt{11}$. Therefore, $Y = Q \cdot 5 = R \cdot 5 \cdot \sqrt{11}$. Therefore, $X = Y \cdot Q = (R \cdot 5 \cdot \sqrt{11}) \cdot (R \cdot \sqrt{11}) = R^2 \cdot 5 \cdot 11 = 5 \cdot 11 \cdot (2 \cdot 7) = 5 \cdot 11 \cdot 2 \cdot 7 = 770$.

\boxed{770}
